string.text: Late-game A-Life accumulates too many active entities. AlifeGuard governs the online population with squad-aware culling, round-robin fairness across factions, and hysteresis to prevent oscillation. Squads survive, smart terrains stay consistent.
Limit active entities around you. Farthest go offline first. Traders, companions, quest targets protected.
How many stalkers and mutants can be active simultaneously. Lower = better framerate in dense areas.
Cull target is max minus buffer. Prevents oscillation: cleanup runs less often because population must rise back above max before the next cycle triggers. 0 disables.
[seconds]\nHow often to check entity counts. Lower = more responsive but slight performance cost. 30 is balanced.
Task NPCs (task givers, bounty and hostage targets) are protected while their task is active. Story characters, traders, named NPCs, and companions are always protected, whether this is on or off.
Cull distant entities first. If disabled, random selection.
Thin squad members before touching commanders. Keeps squads alive, prevents respawn churn. If disabled, all entities released individually like v1.0.
Spread removals evenly across factions and mutant types. If disabled, farthest entities removed regardless of faction.
Show a brief PDA message when the Zone thins out. Messages are lore-flavoured and intentionally vague.
Play a brief psy-flavoured cue when a notification fires. Subtle. Disable for silent notifications.

The online guard only sees entities near you. A hub on another level can hold dozens of offline squads that all come online at once and spike the count. This scan bins offline squads into regions by position and thins any region over the trigger down to the target. Commanders and protected squads are never removed, so every squad survives. Independent of the online guard, with its own trigger, target, and task protection.
Thin overcrowded offline regions on levels away from you. Squads survive, commanders and protected NPCs are never removed.
Offline body count that flags a region as overcrowded. A region is culled only when it holds more bodies than this. Lower = culls more regions.
Body count a flagged region is thinned down to. Lower = heavier culling. 0 strips a region to lone squad commanders. Independent of the online maximum. Clamped to the trigger if set higher.
Task NPCs (task givers, bounty and hostage targets) are protected while their task is active. Story characters, traders, named NPCs, and companions are always protected, whether this is on or off.
[seconds]\nTime between scan steps. Each step examines a small batch of squads, so a full pass over the Zone takes several steps. Lower = fresher density data at slightly higher cost.
Clamps corrupted already_spawned counters on smart terrains. Prevents save crashes and infinite spawn loops caused by spawner and despawner mods racing with the engine on_unregister decrement.

Clamps corrupted already_spawned counters on smart terrains. Negative or non-numeric values cause save crashes (u8 write at smart_terrain.script:955) and infinite spawn loops (max > num always true). Common when running per-NPC despawners that race with the engine's on_unregister decrement. Sanitize runs once on game/level load (before alife pump) and periodically during play. Safe to leave on. Default: on.

Real-time seconds between periodic sanitize passes. Sub-millisecond cost regardless of interval (pure Lua table walk over SIMBOARD.smarts). 300s is a sensible default; lower it only if you observe rapid corruption from another mod. Default: 300.
Debug and development tools. DEBUG enables structured tracing with trace IDs, per-phase timing, and per-entity release logs. Written to alifeguard.log. Has performance cost.

Restore all AlifeGuard MCM settings to factory defaults. Use after upgrading from a version with different MCM structure (the old settings remain as orphaned keys in the MCM storage file but no longer apply).
Warning: save or discard pending MCM changes first; otherwise they override the reset on the next Accept.
Controls log verbosity. ERROR: crashes only. WARN: anomalies. INFO: cycle summaries and config. DEBUG: full tracing with timing per entity.
Zone thinned out. Breathe easier for now.
The Zone reshuffles. Some stalkers walked into the fog.
The Zone exhales. Long-range echoes drop out.
AlifeGuard: all settings restored to defaults.
